gen1.en: It can go for days without eating a single morsel. In the bulb on its back, it stores energy.

gen2.en: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seeds on its back in order to grow.

gen3.en: BULBASAUR can be seen napping in bright sunlight. There is a seed on its back. By soaking up the sun’s rays, the seed grows progressively larger.

gen4.en: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

gen1.en: The bulb on its back grows by drawing energy. It gives off an aroma when it is ready to bloom.

gen2.en: The bulb on its back grows as it absorbs nutrients. The bulb gives off a pleasant aroma when it blooms.

gen3.en: To support its bulb, IVYSAUR’s legs grow sturdy. If it spends more time lying in the sunlight, the bud will soon bloom into a large flower.

gen4.en: When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower’s coming bloom.

gen1.en: The flower on its back catches the sun's rays. The sunlight is then absorbed and used for energy.

gen2.en: As it warms it­ self and absorbs the sunlight, its flower petals release a pleasant fragrance.

gen3.en: VENUSAUR’s flower is said to take on vivid colors if it gets plenty of nutrition and sunlight. The flower’s aroma soothes the emotions of people.

gen4.en: After a rainy day, the flower on its back smells stronger. The scent attracts other Pokémon.

gen1.en: The flame at the tip of its tail makes a sound as it burns. You can only hear it in quiet places.

gen2.en: If it's healthy, the flame on the tip of its tail will burn vigor­ ously, even if it gets a bit wet.

gen3.en: The flame that burns at the tip of its tail is an indication of its emotions. The flame wavers when CHARMANDER is happy, and blazes when it is enraged.

gen4.en: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

gen1.en: Tough fights could excite this POKéMON. When excited, it may blow out bluish- white flames.

gen2.en: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

gen3.en: Without pity, its sharp claws destroy foes. If it encounters a strong enemy, it becomes agitated, and the flame on its tail flares with a bluish white color.

gen4.en: In the rocky mountains where CHARMELEON live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

gen1.en: When expelling a blast of super hot fire, the red flame at the tip of its tail burns more intensely.

gen2.en: It uses its wings to fly high. The temperature of its fire increases as it gains exper­ ience in battle.

gen3.en: A CHARIZARD flies about in search of strong opponents. It breathes intense flames that can melt any material. However, it will never torch a weaker foe.

gen4.en: It is said that CHARIZARD’s fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

gen1.en: Shoots water at prey while in the water. Withdraws into its shell when in danger.

gen2.en: When it feels threatened, it draws its legs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

gen3.en: Its shell is not just for protection. Its rounded shape and the grooves on its surface minimize resistance in water, enabling SQUIRTLE to swim at high speeds.

gen4.en: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

gen1.en: When tapped, this POKéMON will pull in its head, but its tail will still stick out a little bit.

gen2.en: Its long, furry tail is a symbol of longevity, making it quite popular among older people.

gen3.en: Its large tail is covered with rich, thick fur that deepens in color with age. The scratches on its shell are evidence of this POKéMON’s toughness in battle.

gen4.en: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

gen1.en: Once it takes aim at its enemy, it blasts out water with even more force than a fire hose.

gen2.en: It firmly plants its feet on the ground before shooting water from the jets on its back.

gen3.en: The waterspouts that protrude from its shell are highly accurate. Their bullets of water can precisely nail tin cans from a distance of over 160 feet.

gen4.en: The jets of water it spouts from the rocket cannons on its shell can punch through thick steel.

gen1.en: If you touch the feeler on top of its head, it will release a horrible stink to protect itself.

gen2.en: It crawls into foliage where it camouflages itself among leaves that are the same color as its body.

gen3.en: Its voracious appetite compels it to devour leaves bigger than itself without hesitation. It releases a terribly strong odor from its antennae.

gen4.en: It releases a stench from its red antenna to repel enemies. It grows by molting repeatedly.

gen1.en: Hardens its shell to protect itself. However, a large impact may cause it to pop out of its shell.

gen2.en: This is its pre- evolved form. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

gen3.en: Its shell is as hard as an iron slab. A METAPOD does not move very much because it is preparing its soft innards for evolution inside the shell.

gen4.en: A steel-hard shell protects its tender body. It quietly endures hardships while awaiting evolution.

gen1.en: Its wings, covered with poisonous powders, repel water. This allows it to fly in the rain.

gen2.en: It flits from flower to flower, collecting honey. It can even identify distant flowers in bloom.

gen3.en: It has a superior ability to search for delicious honey from flowers. It can seek, extract, and carry honey from flowers blooming over six miles away.

gen4.en: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

gen1.en: Beware of the sharp stinger on its head. It hides in grass and bushes where it eats leaves.

gen2.en: The barb on top of its head secretes a strong poison. It uses this toxic barb to protect itself.

gen3.en: A WEEDLE has an extremely acute sense of smell. It distinguishes its favorite kinds of leaves from those it dislikes by sniffing with its big red proboscis (nose).

gen4.en: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

gen1.en: Able to move only slightly. When endangered, it may stick out its stinger and poison its enemy.

gen2.en: Nearly incapable of movement, it leans against stout trees while waiting for its evolution.

gen3.en: It remains virtually immobile while it clings to a tree. However, on the inside, it busily prepares for evolution. This is evident from how hot its shell becomes.

gen4.en: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

gen1.en: It has 3 poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

gen2.en: It uses sharp, poisonous stings to defeat prey, then takes the victim back to its nest for food.

gen3.en: A BEEDRILL is extremely territorial. For safety reasons, no one should ever approach its nest. If angered, they will attack in a swarm.

gen4.en: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

gen1.en: Very docile. If attacked, it will often kick up sand to protect itself rather than fight back.

gen2.en: It rapidly flaps its wings in the grass, stirring up a dust cloud that drives insect prey out into the open.

gen3.en: It has an extremely sharp sense of direction. It can unerringly return home to its nest, however far it may be removed from its familiar surroundings.

gen4.en: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

gen1.en: This POKéMON is full of vitality. It constantly flies around its large territory in search of prey.

gen2.en: It slowly flies in a circular pat­ tern, all the while keeping a sharp lookout for prey.

gen3.en: This POKéMON flies around, patrolling its large territory. If its living space is violated, it shows no mercy in thoroughly punishing the foe with its sharp claws.

gen4.en: It flies over its wide territory in search of prey, downing it with its highly developed claws.

gen1.en: This POKéMON flies at Mach 2 speed, seeking prey. Its large talons are feared as wicked weapons.

gen2.en: Its outstanding vision allows it to spot splashing MAGIKARP, even while flying at 3300 feet.

gen3.en: This POKéMON has gorgeous, glossy feathers. Many TRAINERS are so captivated by the beautiful feathers on its head that they choose PIDGEOT as their POKéMON.

gen4.en: By flapping its wings with all its might, PIDGEOT can make a gust of wind capable of bending tall trees.

gen1.en: Will chew on any­ thing with its fangs. If you see one, it is certain that 40 more live in the area.

gen2.en: This POKéMON's impressive vital­ ity allows it to live anywhere. It also multiplies very quickly.

gen3.en: A RATTATA is cautious in the extreme. Even while it is asleep, it constantly moves its ears and listens for danger. It will make its nest anywhere.

gen4.en: Cautious in the extreme, its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.

gen1.en: Its hind feet are webbed. They act as flippers, so it can swim in rivers and hunt for prey.

gen2.en: The webs on its hind legs enable it to cross rivers. It search­ es wide areas for food.

gen3.en: A RATICATE’s sturdy fangs grow steadily. To keep them ground down, it gnaws on rocks and logs. It may even chew on the walls of houses.

gen4.en: It whittles its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder walls.
It flaps its small wings busily to fly. Using its beak, it searches in grass for prey.

gen1.en: Inept at flying high. However, it can fly around very fast to protect its ter­ ritory.

gen2.en: To protect its territory, it flies around ceaselessly, making high- pitched cries.

gen3.en: Its loud cry can be heard over half a mile away. If its high, keening cry is heard echoing all around, it is a sign that they are warning each other of danger.

gen1.en: A POKéMON that dates back many years. If it senses danger, it flies high and away, instantly.

gen2.en: It uses its long beak to attack. It has a surprisingly long reach, so it must be treated with caution.

gen3.en: Its long neck and elongated beak are ideal for catching prey in soil or water. It deftly moves this extended and skinny beak to pluck prey.

gen4.en: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

gen1.en: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.

gen2.en: It flutters the tip of its tongue to seek out the scent of prey, then swallows the prey whole.

gen3.en: An EKANS curls itself up in a spiral while it rests. This position allows it to quickly respond to an enemy from any direction with a threat from its upraised head.

gen4.en: It sneaks through grass without making a sound and strikes unsuspecting prey from behind.

gen1.en: The frightening patterns on its belly have been studied. Six variations have been confirmed.

gen2.en: To intimidate foes, it spreads its chest wide and makes eerie sounds by expelling air from its mouth.

gen3.en: This POKéMON has a terrifically strong constricting power. It can even flatten steel oil drums. Once it wraps its body around its foe, escaping is impossible.

gen4.en: The pattern on its belly is for intimidation. It constricts foes while they are frozen in fear.

gen1.en: It keeps its tail raised to monitor its surroundings. If you yank its tail, it will try to bite you.

gen2.en: When it is anger­ ed, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

gen3.en: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

gen4.en: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow PIKACHU that is in a weakened state.

gen8.en: Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.

gen1.en: When electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes feisty. It also glows in the dark.

gen2.en: If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electri­ city from the atmosphere.

gen3.en: If it stores too much electricity, its behavior turns aggressive. To avoid this, it occasionally discharges excess energy and calms itself down.

gen4.en: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

gen8.en: It can discharge bursts of electricity exceeding 100,000 volts— a single strike with that amount of power would incapacitate one of the Copperajah of my homeland.

gen1.en: Its body is dry. When it gets cold at night, its hide is said to become coated with a fine dew.

gen2.en: It prefers dry, sandy places because it uses the sand to protect itself when threatened.

gen3.en: When it curls up in a ball, it can make any attack bounce off harmlessly. Its hide has turned tough and solid as a result of living in the desert.

gen4.en: To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

gen1.en: It is skilled at slashing enemies with its claws. If broken, they start to grow back in a day.

gen2.en: Adept at climbing trees, it rolls into a spiny ball, then attacks its enemies from above.

gen3.en: It curls up in a ball to protect itself from enemy attacks. It also curls up to prevent heatstroke during the daytime when temperatures rise sharply.

gen4.en: It curls up, then rolls into foes with its back. Its sharp spines inflict severe damage.

gen1.en: A mild-mannered POKéMON that does not like to fight. Beware, its small horns secrete venom.

gen2.en: Small and very docile, it pro­ tects itself with its small, poison­ ous horn when attacked.

gen3.en: Its highly toxic barbs are thought to have developed as protection for this small- bodied POKéMON. When enraged, it releases a horrible toxin from its horn.

gen4.en: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from barbs can be fatal.

gen1.en: When resting deep in its burrow, its thorns always retract. This is proof that it is relaxed.

gen2.en: It has a docile nature. If it is threatened with attack, it raises the barbs that are all over its body.

gen3.en: When it is with its friends or family, its barbs are tucked away to prevent injury. It appears to become nervous if separated from the others.

gen4.en: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than NIDORINO’s.

gen1.en: Tough scales cover the sturdy body of this POKéMON. It appears that the scales grow in cycles.

gen2.en: The hard scales that cover its strong body serve as excellent protection from any attack.

gen3.en: It is adept at sending foes flying with harsh tackles using its tough, scaly body. This POKéMON is at its strongest when it is defending its young.

gen4.en: Its entire body is armored with hard scales. It will protect the young in its burrow with its life.

gen1.en: Its large ears are always kept upright. If it senses danger, it will attack with a poisonous sting.

gen2.en: It constantly moves its large ears in many directions in order to detect danger right away.

gen3.en: The male NIDORAN has developed muscles that freely move its ears in any direction. Even the slightest sound does not escape this POKéMON’s notice.

gen4.en: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

gen1.en: Its horns contain venom. If they are stabbed into an enemy, the impact makes the poison leak out.

gen2.en: It is easily agitated and uses its horn for offense as soon as it notices an attacker.

gen3.en: Its horn is harder than a diamond. If it senses a hostile presence, all the barbs on its back bristle up at once, and it challenges the foe with all its might.

gen4.en: It has a violent disposition and stabs foes with its horn, which oozes poison upon impact.

gen1.en: Its steel-like hide adds to its powerful tackle. Its horns are so hard, they can pierce a diamond.

gen2.en: It uses its thick arms, legs and tail to attack forcefully. Melee combat is its specialty.

gen3.en: A NIDOKING’s thick tail packs enormously destructive power capable of toppling a metal transmission tower. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

gen4.en: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

gen1.en: Adored for their cute looks and playfulness. They are thought to be rare, as they do not appear often.

gen2.en: Though rarely seen, it becomes easier to spot, for some reason, on the night of a full moon.

gen3.en: On every night of a full moon, they come out to play. When dawn arrives, the tired CLEFAIRY go to sleep nestled up against each other in deep and quiet mountains.

gen4.en: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of CLEFAIRY dancing under a full moon.

gen8.en: It can be found in quiet mountain areas on a full moon's night. Its dancing and its tiny, faintly glowing wings confer upon it a lovely fairylike quality.

gen1.en: They appear to be very protective of their own world. It is a kind of fairy, rarely seen by people.

gen2.en: Said to live in quiet, remote mountains, this type of fairy has a strong aversion to being seen.

gen3.en: A CLEFABLE uses its wings to skip lightly as if it were flying. Its bouncy step lets it even walk on water. On quiet, moonlit nights, it strolls on lakes.

gen4.en: It is very wary and rarely shows itself to people. Its ears can hear a pin drop over half a mile away.

gen8.en: Legend says that on clear, quiet nights, it listens for the voices of its kin living on the moon. I, too, often think of my homeland, so far away.

gen1.en: Both its fur and its tails are beautiful. As it grows, the tails split and form more tails.

gen2.en: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxur­ ious coat of fur.

gen3.en: It can freely control fire, making fiery orbs fly like will-o’-the-wisps. Just before evolution, its six tails grow hot as if on fire.

gen4.en: It controls balls of fire. As it grows, its six tails split from their tips to make more tails.

gen8.en: In its belly burns a fire, which Vulpix spits out in the form of fireballs. When young, this Pokémon has but one white tail. As the Pokémon matures, this single tail splits into six.

gen1.en: According to an enduring legend, 9 noble saints were united and reincarnated as this POKéMON.

gen2.en: It is said to live a thousand years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

gen3.en: It has long been said that each of the nine tails embody an enchanted power. A long-lived NINETALES will have fur that shines like gold.

gen4.en: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

gen8.en: The coat of gleaming golden fur is quite magnificent. This species is said to store sacred power in its nine long tails and to live for a millennium.

gen1.en: Uses its alluring eyes to enrapture its foe. It then sings a pleasing melody that lulls the foe to sleep.

gen2.en: It rolls its cute eyes as it sings a soothing lullaby. Its gentle song puts anyone who hears it to sleep.

gen3.en: Nothing can avoid falling asleep hearing a JIGGLYPUFF’s song. The sound waves of its singing voice match the brain waves of someone in a deep sleep.

gen4.en: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

gen1.en: Its body is full of elasticity. By inhaling deeply, it can continue to inflate itself without limit.

gen2.en: The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can't stop touching it.

gen3.en: Its fur is the ultimate in luxuriousness. Sleeping alongside a WIGGLYTUFF is simply divine. Its body expands seemingly without end when it inhales.

gen4.en: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

gen1.en: Emits ultrasonic cries while it flies. They act as a sonar used to check for ob­ jects in its way.

gen2.en: During the day, it gathers with others and hangs from the ceilings of old buildings and caves.

gen3.en: While living in pitch-black caverns, their eyes gradually grew shut and deprived them of vision. They use ultrasonic waves to detect obstacles.

gen4.en: It checks its surroundings and location using reflections of the ultrasonic waves from its mouth.

gen8.en: Makes its home in gloomy caves. Atrophied eyes have left this Pokémon blind, so it scans its surroundings via sound waves that it emits from its mouth as it flies.

gen1.en: It attacks in a stealthy manner, without warning. Its sharp fangs are used to bite and suck blood.

gen2.en: When it plunges its fangs into its prey, it instantly draws and gulps down more than ten ounces of blood.

gen3.en: Its fangs easily puncture even thick animal hide. It loves to feast on the blood of people and POKéMON. It flits about in darkness and strikes from behind.

gen4.en: Its sharp fangs puncture the toughest of hides and have small holes for greedily sucking blood.

gen8.en: It sinks its sharp fangs into other creatures and slurps up their blood. A closer look at the fangs reveals that they are hollow and akin to straws.

gen1.en: It may be mistaken for a clump of weeds. If you try to yank it out of the ground, it shrieks horribly.

gen2.en: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bath­ ing in moonlight.

gen3.en: This POKéMON grows by absorbing moonlight. During the daytime, it buries itself in the ground, leaving only its leaves exposed to avoid detection by its enemies.

gen4.en: It often plants its root feet in the ground during the day and sows seeds as it walks about at night.

gen1.en: Smells incredibly foul! However, around 1 out of 1,000 people enjoy sniffing its nose- bending stink.

gen2.en: The smell from its drool-like syrup and the pollen on its petals is so bad, it may make opponents faint.

gen3.en: A horribly noxious honey drools from its mouth. One whiff of the honey can result in memory loss. Some fans are said to enjoy this overwhelming stink, however.

gen4.en: The honey it drools from its mouth smells so atrocious, it can curl noses more than a mile away.

gen1.en: Flaps its broad flower petals to scatter its poisonous pollen. The flapping sound is very loud.

gen2.en: By shaking its big petals, it scat­ ters toxic pollen into the air, turning the air yellow.

gen3.en: In seasons when it produces more pollen, the air around a VILEPLUME turns yellow with the powder as it walks. The pollen is highly toxic and causes paralysis.

gen4.en: Its petals are the largest in the world. As it walks, it scatters extremely allergenic pollen.

gen1.en: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

gen2.en: The tochukaso growing on this POKéMON's back orders it to extract juice from tree trunks.

gen3.en: A PARAS has parasitic tochukaso mushrooms growing on its back. They grow by drawing nutrients from the host. They are valued as a medicine for long life.

gen4.en: Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.

gen8.en: Sometimes seen at the foot of trees in humid forests. The mushrooms on its back—called tochukaso—are not present on infant specimens and instead emerge as Paras matures.

gen1.en: The bug host is drained of energy by the mushrooms on its back. They appear to do all the thinking.

gen2.en: When nothing's left to extract from the bug, the mushrooms on its back leave spores on the bug's egg.

gen3.en: PARASECT are known to infest the roots of large trees en masse and drain nutrients. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.

gen4.en: A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.

gen8.en: Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.

gen1.en: Its large eyes act as radars. In a bright place, you can see that they are clusters of many tiny eyes.

gen2.en: The small bugs it eats appear only at night, so it sleeps in a hole in a tree until night falls.

gen3.en: Its coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body is said to have evolved for protection. Its large eyes never fail to spot even miniscule prey.

gen4.en: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

gen1.en: The powdery scales on its wings are hard to remove. They also contain poison that leaks out on contact.

gen2.en: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

gen3.en: VENOMOTH are nocturnal--they only are active at night. Their favorite prey are insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.

gen4.en: It flutters its wings to scatter dustlike scales. The scales leach toxins if they contact skin.

gen1.en: It prefers dark places. It spends most of its time underground, though it may pop up in caves.

gen2.en: It digs under­ ground and chews on tree roots, sticking its head out only when the sun isn't bright.

gen3.en: DIGLETT are raised in most farms. The reason is simple--wherever they burrow, the soil is left perfectly tilled for growing delicious crops.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that lives underground. Because of its dark habitat, it is repelled by bright sunlight.

gen1.en: A team of triplets that can burrow over 60 MPH. Due to this, some people think it's an earthquake.

gen2.en: These DIGLETT triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it's like underground.

gen3.en: Because the triplets originally split from one body, they think exactly alike. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly through the ground.

gen4.en: Its three heads move alternately, driving it through tough soil to depths of over 60 miles.

gen1.en: Appears to be more active at night. It loves round and shiny things. It can't stop from picking them up.

gen2.en: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines too.

gen3.en: MEOWTH withdraw their sharp claws into their paws to silently sneak about. For some reason, this POKéMON loves shiny coins that glitter with light.

gen4.en: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

gen1.en: The gem in its forehead glows on its own! It walks with all the grace and elegance of a proud queen.

gen2.en: Behind its lithe, elegant appearance lies a barbaric side. It will tear apart its prey on a mere whim.

gen3.en: A PERSIAN’s six bold whiskers sense air movements to determine what is in its vicinity. It becomes docile if grabbed by the whiskers.

gen4.en: A very haughty Pokémon. Among fans, the size of the jewel in its forehead is a topic of much talk.

gen1.en: Always tormented by headaches. It uses psychic powers, but it is not known if it intends to do so.

gen2.en: The only time it can use its psy­ chic power is when its sleeping brain cells happen to wake.

gen3.en: When its headache intensifies, it starts using strange powers. However, it has no recollection of its powers, so it always looks befuddled and bewildered.

gen4.en: Overwhelmed by enigmatic abilities, it suffers a constant headache. It sometimes uses mysterious powers.

gen8.en: Suffers perpetual headaches. If the agony grows too great, Psyduck's latent power erupts, contrary to Psyduck's intent. Ergo, I am exploring ways to ease the pain.

gen1.en: Its slim and long limbs end in broad flippers. They are used for swim­ ming gracefully in lakes.

gen2.en: It swims grace­ fully along on the quiet, slow-moving rivers and lakes of which it is so fond.

gen3.en: A GOLDUCK is an adept swimmer. It sometimes joins competitive swimmers in training. It uses psychic powers when its forehead shimmers with light.

gen4.en: It is seen swimming dynamically and elegantly using its well-developed limbs and flippers.

gen8.en: Its body is strong, and it has webbing on its hands and feet. Golduck can swim easily through rough seas, clawing its way through the high waves.

gen1.en: An agile POKéMON that lives in trees. It angers easily and will not hesitate to attack anything.

gen2.en: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

gen3.en: When it starts shaking and its nasal breathing turns rough, it’s a sure sign of anger. However, since this happens instantly, there is no time to flee.

gen4.en: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

evolutions.condition: Use Rage Fist 20 times and level-up

gen1.en: It stops being angry only when nobody else is around. To view this moment is very difficult.

gen2.en: It will beat up anyone who makes it mad, even if it has to chase them until the end of the world.

gen3.en: When it becomes furious, its blood circulation becomes more robust, and its muscles are made stronger. But it also becomes much less intelligent.

gen4.en: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

gen1.en: A POKéMON with a friendly nature. However, it will bark fiercely at anything invading its territory.

gen2.en: It controls a big territory. If it detects an unknown smell, it roars loudly to force out the intruder.

gen3.en: Its superb sense of smell ensures that this POKéMON won’t forget any scent, no matter what. It uses its sense of smell to detect the emotions of others.

gen4.en: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

gen8.en: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

gen1.en: A legendary POKé­ MON in China. Many people are charmed by its grace and beauty while running.

gen2.en: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were attracted to its movement as it ran through prairies.

gen3.en: This fleet-footed POKéMON is said to run over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within its body is its source of power.

gen4.en: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

gen8.en: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

gen1.en: The direction of the spiral on the belly differs by area. It is more adept at swimming than walking.

gen2.en: The swirl on its belly is its insides showing through the skin. It looks clearer after it eats.

gen3.en: It is possible to see this POKéMON’s spiral innards right through its thin skin. However, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.

gen4.en: Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.

gen1.en: Under attack, it uses its belly spiral to put the foe to sleep. It then makes its escape.

gen2.en: Though it is skilled at walk­ ing, it prefers to live underwater where there is less danger.

gen3.en: Its body surface is always wet and slick with an oily fluid. Because of this greasy covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.

gen4.en: The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.

gen1.en: Swims powerfully using all the muscles in its body. It can even overtake champion swimmers.

gen2.en: It can use its well-developed arms and legs to run on the surface of the water for a split second.

gen3.en: Its highly developed muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. This POKéMON can swim back and forth across the Pacific Ocean without effort.

gen4.en: With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.

gen1.en: Sleeps 18 hours a day. If it senses danger, it will teleport itself to safety even as it sleeps.

gen2.en: It hypnotizes itself so that it can teleport away when it senses danger, even if it is asleep.

gen3.en: A POKéMON that sleeps 18 hours a day. Observation revealed that it uses TELEPORT to change its location once every hour.

gen4.en: Using its psychic power is such a strain on its brain that it needs to sleep for 18 hours a day.

gen8.en: Spends 18 hours of the day sleeping. Even while asleep, Abra can control its psychic powers—should danger approach, the Pokémon will simply teleport away.

gen1.en: Many odd things happen if this POKéMON is close by. For example, it makes clocks run backwards.

gen2.en: When it closes its eyes, twice as many alpha parti­ cles come out of the surface of its body.

gen3.en: It is rumored that a boy with psychic abilities suddenly transformed into KADABRA while he was assisting research into extrasensory powers.

gen4.en: It stares at its silver spoon to focus its mind. It emits more alpha waves while doing so.

gen8.en: There are rumors that a child with mystical powers became a Kadabra; however, this remains unverified. I suspect that the spoon Kadabra holds enhances its brain waves.

gen1.en: A POKéMON that can memorize anything. It never forgets what it learns-- that's why this POKéMON is smart.

gen2.en: It has an IQ of 5000. It calcu­ lates many things in order to gain the edge in every battle.

gen3.en: While it has strong psychic abilities and high intelligence, an ALAKAZAM’s muscles are very weak. It uses psychic power to move its body.

gen4.en: The spoons clutched in its hands are said to have been created by its psychic powers.

gen8.en: The longer Alakazam lives, the larger and heavier its head becomes. Our tests have shown that the strength of its psychic powers correlates positively to the weight of its head.

gen1.en: Very powerful in spite of its small size. Its mastery of many types of martial arts makes it very tough.

gen2.en: It trains by lifting rocks in the mountains. It can even pick up a GRAVELER with ease.

gen3.en: It continually undertakes strenuous training to master all forms of martial arts. Its strength lets it easily hoist a sumo wrestler onto its shoulders.

gen4.en: Though small in stature, it is powerful enough to easily heft and throw a number of GEODUDE at once.

gen8.en: Though as small as a child, it has strength enough to easily throw a well-built adult. Striving to become ever stronger, Machop trains by carrying a Graveler on its shoulders.

gen1.en: The belt around its waist holds back its energy. Without it, this POKéMON would be unstoppable.

gen2.en: This tough POKéMON always stays in the zone. Its muscles become thicker after every battle.

gen3.en: A belt is worn by a MACHOKE to keep its overwhelming power under control. Because it is so dangerous, no one has ever removed the belt.

gen4.en: It happily carries heavy cargo to toughen up. It willingly does hard work for people.

gen8.en: A sturdy creature boasting a robust physique and boundless stamina. Loves training above all else and voluntarily assists with tasks such as construction and clearing land.

gen1.en: One arm alone can move mountains. Using all four arms, this POKéMON fires off awesome punches.

gen2.en: With four arms that react more quickly than it can think, it can execute many punches at once.

gen3.en: It is impossible to defend against punches and chops doled out by its four arms. Its fighting spirit flares up when it faces a tough opponent.

gen4.en: Its four muscled arms slam foes with powerful punches and chops at blinding speed.

gen8.en: In close combat, its four arms afford it offensive and defensive supremacy. In but a blink, this valiant Pokémon can overwhelm its foes with more than 1,000 blows from its fists.

gen1.en: Prefers hot and humid places. It ensnares tiny insects with its vines and devours them.

gen2.en: If it notices anything that moves, it immediately flings its vine at the object.

gen3.en: A BELLSPROUT’s thin and flexible body lets it bend and sway to avoid any attack, however strong it may be. From its mouth, it leaks a fluid that melts even iron.

gen4.en: It prefers hot and humid environments. It is quick at capturing prey with its vines.

gen1.en: When hungry, it swallows anything that moves. Its hapless prey is melted inside by strong acids.

gen2.en: When it's hungry, it swings its razor-sharp leaves, slicing up any unlucky object nearby for food.

gen3.en: At night, a WEEPINBELL hangs on to a tree branch with its hooked rear and sleeps. If it moves around in its sleep, it may wake up to find itself on the ground.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that appears to be a plant. It captures unwary prey by dousing them with a toxic powder.

gen1.en: Lures prey with the sweet aroma of honey. Swallowed whole, the prey is melted in a day, bones and all.

gen2.en: Once ingested into this POKéMON's body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

gen3.en: The long vine extending from its head is waved about as if it were a living thing to attract prey. When an unsuspecting victim approaches, it is swallowed whole.

gen4.en: It pools in its mouth a fluid with a honeylike scent, which is really an acid that dissolves anything.

gen1.en: It can sometimes be found all dry and shriveled up on a beach. Toss it back into the sea to revive it.

gen2.en: As it floats along on the waves, it uses its toxic feelers to stab anything it touches.

gen3.en: Its body is almost entirely composed of water. It ensnares its foe with its two long tentacles, then stabs with the poison stingers at their tips.

gen4.en: Because its body is almost entirely composed of water, it shrivels up if it is washed ashore.

gen8.en: They fire beams from the glassy, magenta orbs that resemble eyes atop their heads, and they drift in shallow seas. During low tide, they can sometimes be found on beaches, desiccated.

gen1.en: Its 80 tentacles can stretch and contract freely. They wrap around prey and weaken it with poison.

gen2.en: When its 80 feel­ ers absorb water, it stretches to become like a net to entangle its prey.

gen3.en: It lives in complex rock formations on the ocean floor and traps prey using its 80 tentacles. Its red orbs glow when it grows excited or agitated.

gen4.en: It extends its 80 tentacles to form an encircling poisonous net that is difficult to escape.

gen8.en: It has 80 tentacles, each with a venomous tip. These tentacles are also extendible, lengthening when Tentacruel attempts to catch prey. Use caution.

gen1.en: Commonly found near mountain trails, etc. If you step on one by accident, it gets angry.

gen2.en: Proud of their sturdy bodies, they bash against each other in a contest to prove whose is harder.

gen3.en: It climbs mountain paths using only the power of its arms. Because they look just like boulders lining paths, hikers may step on them without noticing.

gen4.en: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.

gen8.en: Makes its home in mountainous regions, using its arms to climb along harsh mountain roads. Can be troublesome—carelessly kicking one will cause it to fly into a rage and chase after you.

gen1.en: Often seen rolling down mountain trails. Obstacles are just things to roll straight over, not avoid.

gen2.en: It travels by rol­ ling on mountain paths. If it gains too much speed, it stops by running into huge rocks.

gen3.en: They descend from mountains by tumbling down steep slopes. They are so brutal, they smash aside obstructing trees and massive boulders with thunderous tackles.

gen4.en: It rolls on mountain paths to move. Once it builds momentum, no Pokémon can stop it without difficulty.

gen8.en: Dwells in holes dug into sheer walls of stone. It enjoys rolling down slopes as though it were a boulder during a rockfall, so keep an eye upward while traversing mountain roads.

gen1.en: Once it sheds its skin, its body turns tender and whitish. Its hide hardens when it's exposed to air.

gen2.en: Its rock-like body is so durable, even high-powered dynamite blasts fail to scratch its rugged hide.

gen3.en: It is said to live in volcanic craters on mountain peaks. Once a year, it sheds its hide and grows larger. The shed hide crumbles and returns to the soil.

gen4.en: Even dynamite can’t harm its hard, boulderlike body. It sheds its hide just once a year.

gen8.en: The rocklike shell is shed each year. The cast-off shell then crumbles, reverting to a mass of soil, which can be spread across fields to promote crop growth.

gen1.en: Capable of jumping incredibly high. Its hooves and sturdy legs absorb the impact of a hard landing.

gen2.en: Training by jumping over grass that grows longer every day has made it a world-class jumper.

gen3.en: A PONYTA is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. Its legs become stronger as it stumbles and falls while trying to keep up with its parent.

gen4.en: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

gen8.en: These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.

gen1.en: Just loves to run. If it sees some­ thing faster than itself, it will give chase at top speed.

gen2.en: It just loves to gallop. The faster it goes, the long­ er the swaying flames of its mane will become.

gen3.en: It usually canters casually in the fields and plains. But once a RAPIDASH turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.

gen4.en: When at an all-out gallop, its blazing mane sparkles, enhancing its beautiful appearance.

gen8.en: Fiery mane aglow, Rapidash darts like an arrow across the land. This prodigiously swift creature can traverse the vast region of Hisui in a day and a half.

gen1.en: Incredibly slow and sluggish. It is quite content to loll about without worrying about the time.

gen2.en: It is always so absent-minded that it won't react, even if its flavorful tail is bitten.

gen3.en: It catches prey by dipping its tail in water at the side of a river. But it often forgets what it is doing and spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.

gen4.en: Although slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.

gen1.en: Lives lazily by the sea. If the SHELLDER on its tail comes off, it becomes a SLOWPOKE again.

gen2.en: An attached SHELLDER won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.

gen3.en: Its tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This forces it to reluctantly swim and catch prey.

gen4.en: Though usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.

gen1.en: It is born with the ability to defy gravity. It floats in air on powerful electro­ magnetic waves.

gen2.en: The electricity emitted by the units on each side of its body cause it to become a strong magnet.

gen3.en: The units at its sides are extremely powerful magnets. They generate enough magnetism to draw in iron objects from over 300 feet away.

gen4.en: The faster the units at its sides rotate, the greater the magnetic force they generate.

gen8.en: A bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.

gen1.en: Generates strange radio signals. It raises the tem­ perature by 3.6F degrees within 3,300 feet.

gen2.en: When many MAGNETON gather together, the resulting magnetic storm disrupts radio waves.

gen3.en: It is actually three MAGNEMITE linked by magnetism. It generates powerful radio waves that raise temperatures by 3.6 degrees F within a 3,300-foot radius.

gen4.en: Many mysteriously appear when more sunspots dot the sun. They stop TV sets from displaying properly.

gen8.en: Three Magnemite gathered to evolve into this Pokémon. The source of much vexation on my part, as its powerful magnetism destroys my research equipment.

gen1.en: Lives where reedy plants grow. They are rarely seen, so it's thought their numbers are decreasing.

gen2.en: In order to pre­ vent their extinction, more people have made an effort to breed these POKéMON.

gen3.en: It is always seen with a stick from a plant. Apparently, there are good sticks and bad sticks. This POKéMON occasionally fights with others over choice sticks.

gen4.en: It can’t live without the stalk it holds. That’s why it defends the stalk from attackers with its life.

gen1.en: Its short wings make flying dif­ ficult. Instead, this POKéMON runs at high speed on developed legs.

gen2.en: It lives on a grassy plain where it can see a long way. If it sees an enemy, it runs away at 60 mph.

gen3.en: Even while eating or sleeping, one of the heads remains always vigilant for any sign of danger. When threatened, it flees at over 60 miles per hour.

gen4.en: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

gen1.en: One of DODUO's 2 heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 MPH in prairies.

gen2.en: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads--even for a second--will get pecked severely.

gen3.en: A peculiar POKéMON species with three heads. It vigorously races across grassy plains even in arid seasons with little rainfall.

gen4.en: When DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

gen1.en: Loves freezing cold conditions. Relishes swimming in a frigid cli­ mate of around 14F degrees.

gen2.en: The light blue fur that covers it keeps it protected against the cold. It loves iceberg- filled oceans.

gen3.en: SEEL hunt for prey in frigid, ice-covered seas. When it needs to breathe, it punches a hole through the ice with the sharply protruding section of its head.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.

gen1.en: Its entire body is a snowy-white. Unharmed by even intense cold, it swims powerfully in icy waters.

gen2.en: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's cold.

gen3.en: It loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this POKéMON sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.

gen4.en: In snow, the pure white coat covering its body obscures it from predators.

gen1.en: Made of hardened sludge. It smells too putrid to touch. Even weeds won't grow in its path.

gen2.en: When two of these POKéMON's bodies are combined together, new poisons are created.

gen3.en: Born from polluted sludge in the sea, GRIMER’s favorite food is anything filthy. They feed on wastewater pumped out from factories.

gen4.en: It was born when sludge in a dirty stream was exposed to the moon’s X-rays. It appears among filth.

gen1.en: Smells so awful, it can cause fainting. Through degeneration, it lost its sense of smell.

gen2.en: As it moves, a very strong poison leaks from it, making the ground there barren for three years.

gen3.en: It prefers warm and humid habitats. In the summertime, the toxic substances in its body intensify, making MUK reek like putrid kitchen garbage.

gen4.en: A toxic fluid seeps from its body. The fluid instantly kills plants and trees on contact.

gen1.en: The shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.

gen2.en: Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.

gen3.en: At night, it burrows a hole in the seafloor with its broad tongue to make a place to sleep. While asleep, it closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.

gen4.en: It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.

gen1.en: For protection, it uses its harder- than-diamonds shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.

gen2.en: Even a missile can't break the spikes it uses to stab opponents. They're even hard­ er than its shell.

gen3.en: It swims in the sea by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. The CLOYSTER shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.

gen4.en: It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.

gen1.en: Said to appear in decrepit, deserted buildings. It has no real shape as it appears to be made of a gas.

gen2.en: It wraps its op­ ponent in its gas- like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

gen3.en: When exposed to a strong wind, a GASTLY’s gaseous body quickly dwindles away. They cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.

gen4.en: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

gen8.en: Gaseous and completely impalpable. Also highly dangerous— inhaling part of its poisonous body will cause one to faint instantly.

gen1.en: By licking, it saps the victim's life. It causes shaking that won't stop until the victim's demise.

gen2.en: It hides in the dark, planning to take the life of the next living thing that wanders close by.

gen3.en: If a HAUNTER beckons you while it is floating in darkness, don’t approach it. This POKéMON will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.

gen4.en: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

gen8.en: This frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter's lick will wither to death day by day.

gen1.en: A GENGAR is close by if you feel a sudden chill. It may be trying to lay a curse on you.

gen2.en: Hiding in people's shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.

gen3.en: Deep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a GENGAR running past you, pretending to be your shadow.

gen4.en: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a GENGAR delighting in casting curses on people.

gen8.en: Possesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!

gen1.en: Burrows at high speed in search of food. The tunnels it leaves are used as homes by DIGLETTs.

gen2.en: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

gen3.en: There is a magnet in its brain that prevents an ONIX from losing direction while tunneling. As it grows older, its body becomes steadily rounder and smoother.

gen4.en: It burrows through the ground at a speed of 50 mph while feeding on large boulders.

gen8.en: This chain of immense stones resembles a giant serpent. Tremors shake the earth above as it burrows deep beneath the ground, feeding on boulders as it goes.

gen1.en: If you sleep by it all the time, it will sometimes show you dreams it has eaten in the past.

gen2.en: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

gen3.en: If your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it’s a sure sign that a DROWZEE is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.

gen4.en: It can tell what people are dreaming by sniffing with its big nose. It loves fun dreams.

gen1.en: Avoid eye contact if you come across one. It will try to put you to sleep by using its pendulum.

gen2.en: The longer it swings its pendulum, the longer the effects of its hypnosis last.

gen3.en: The arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum in a HYPNO’s hand lull the foe into deep hypnosis. While searching for prey, it polishes the pendulum.

gen4.en: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.

gen1.en: Its pincers are superb weapons. They sometimes break off during battle, but they grow back fast.

gen2.en: If it is unable to find food, it will absorb nutrients by swallowing a mouthful of sand.

gen3.en: KRABBY live in holes dug into beaches. On sandy shores with little in the way of food, they can be seen squabbling with each other over territory.

gen4.en: It lives in burrows dug on sandy beaches. Its pincers fully grow back if they are broken in battle.

gen1.en: One claw grew massively and as hard as steel. It has 10,000-HP strength. However, it is too heavy.

gen2.en: Its oversized claw is very powerful, but when it's not in battle, the claw just gets in the way.

gen3.en: It waves its huge, oversized claw in the air to communicate with others. But since the claw is so heavy, this POKéMON quickly tires.

gen4.en: The larger pincer has 10,000- horsepower strength. However, it is so heavy, it is difficult to aim.

gen1.en: It is said to camouflage itself as a POKé BALL. It will self-destruct with very little stimulus.

gen2.en: During the study of this POKéMON, it was discovered that its compo­ nents are not found in nature.

gen3.en: It bears an uncanny and unexplained resemblance to a POKé BALL. Because it explodes at the slightest shock, even veteran TRAINERS treat it with caution.

gen4.en: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

gen8.en: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

gen1.en: Stores electrical energy inside its body. Even the slightest shock could trigger a huge explosion.

gen2.en: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

gen3.en: They appear in great numbers at electric power plants. Because they feed on electricity, they cause massive and chaotic blackouts in nearby cities.

gen4.en: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

gen8.en: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.

gen1.en: The heads attract each other and spin around. There must be 6 heads for it to maintain balance.

gen2.en: If even one is separated from the group, the energy bond between the six will make them rejoin instantly.

gen3.en: It consists of six eggs that care for each other. The eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear, it is close to evolution.

gen4.en: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

gen1.en: Its cries are very noisy. This is because each of the 3 heads thinks about whatever it likes.

gen2.en: Living in a good environment makes it grow lots of heads. A head that drops off becomes an EXEGGCUTE.

gen3.en: Originally from the tropics, EXEGGUTOR’s heads grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall, they group to form an EXEGGCUTE.

gen4.en: It is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an EXEGGCUTE.

gen1.en: Wears the skull of its deceased mother. Its cries echo inside the skull and come out as a sad melody.

gen2.en: It lost its mother after its birth. It wears its mother's skull, never revealing its true face.

gen3.en: It pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull it wears are from its tears.

gen4.en: When it thinks of its dead mother, it cries. Its crying makes the skull it wears rattle hollowly.

gen1.en: Small and weak, this POKéMON is adept with its bone club. It has grown more vicious over the ages.

gen2.en: Somewhere in the world is a ceme­ tery just for MAROWAK. It gets its bones from those graves.

gen3.en: A MAROWAK is the evolved form of a CUBONE that has grown tough by overcoming the grief of losing its mother. Its tempered and hardened spirit is not easily broken.

gen4.en: From its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.

gen1.en: When kicking, the sole of its foot turns as hard as a diamond on im­ pact and destroys its enemy.

gen2.en: It is also called the Kick Master. It uses its elastic legs to execute every known kick.

gen3.en: Its legs freely stretch and contract. Using these springlike limbs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its tired legs.

gen4.en: Its legs can stretch double. First-time foes are startled by its extensible reach.

gen1.en: Punches in cork­ screw fashion. It can punch its way through a concrete wall in the same way as a drill.

gen2.en: To increase the strength of all its punch moves, it spins its arms just before making contact.

gen3.en: A HITMONCHAN is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who aimed to become the world champion. Having an indomitable spirit means that it will never give up.

gen4.en: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

gen1.en: Its tongue spans almost 7 feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.

gen2.en: It has a tongue that is over 6’6’’ long. It uses this long tongue to lick its body clean.

gen3.en: Whenever it sees something unfamiliar, it always licks the object because it memorizes things by texture and taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.

gen4.en: When it extends its over-six-foot- long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.

gen8.en: Wields its long tongue deftly, as though it were an arm. The Pokémon's viscous saliva, once it has been collected and boiled down, yields a strong and highly useful adhesive.

gen1.en: In hot places, its internal gases could expand and explode without any warning. Be very careful!

gen2.en: If one gets close enough to it when it expels poison­ ous gas, the gas swirling inside it can be seen.

gen3.en: Getting up close to a KOFFING will give you a chance to observe, through its thin skin, the toxic gases swirling inside. It blows up at the slightest stimulation.

gen4.en: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

gen1.en: It lives and grows by absorbing dust, germs and poison gases that are contained in toxic waste and garbage.

gen2.en: When it inhales poisonous gases from garbage, its body expands, and its insides smell much worse.

gen3.en: By diluting its toxic gases with a special process, the highest grade of perfume can be extracted. To WEEZING, gases emanating from garbage are the ultimate feast.

gen4.en: It grows by feeding on gases released by garbage. Though very rare, triplets have been found.

gen1.en: A POKéMON with a one-track mind. Once it charges, it won't stop running until it falls asleep.

gen2.en: It can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.

gen3.en: Once it starts running, it doesn’t stop. Its tiny brain makes it so stupid that it can’t remember why it started running in the first place.

gen4.en: Its powerful tackles can destroy anything. However, it is too slow witted to help people work.

gen8.en: Ludicrously strong—when it butts heads with a mountain, it is the mountain that shatters. But its short legs struggle with turns, and it is incapable of stopping unless it collides with something.

gen1.en: Walks on its hind legs. Shows signs of intelligence. Its armor-like hide even repels molten lava.

gen2.en: By lightly spin­ ning its drill- like horn, it can easily shatter even a diamond in the rough.

gen3.en: Its horn, which rotates like a drill, destroys tall buildings with one strike. It stands on its hind legs, and its brain is well developed.

gen4.en: Standing on its hind legs freed its forelegs and made it smarter. It is very forgetful, however.

gen8.en: Rapidly rotates its horn to bore through bedrock. It swaggers around volcanic regions, protected from the lava's heat by its tough, armorlike hide.

gen1.en: A gentle and kind­ hearted POKéMON that shares its nutritious eggs if it sees an injured POKéMON.

gen2.en: People try to catch it for its extremely nutritious eggs, but it rarely can be found.

gen3.en: CHANSEY lay nutritionally excellent eggs every day. The eggs are so delicious, they are eagerly devoured by even those people who have lost their appetite.

gen4.en: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

gen8.en: This purehearted Pokémon shares its eggs with the injured.

gen1.en: Its identity is obscured by masses of thick, blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.

gen2.en: During battle, it constantly moves the vines that cover its body in order to annoy its opponent.

gen3.en: Its vines snap off easily and painlessly if they are grabbed, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by new growth the very next day.

gen4.en: The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.

gen8.en: It is cloaked entirely in blue vines, preventing any glimpse of its true identity. The vines impart a refreshing sensation when chewed—they're useful as a spice.

gen1.en: Raises its young in its belly pouch. Won't run from any fight to keep its young protected.

gen2.en: To avoid crushing the baby it carries in its pouch, it always sleeps standing up.

gen3.en: If you come across a young KANGASKHAN playing by itself, never try to catch it. The baby’s parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged.

gen4.en: It raises its offspring in its belly pouch. It lets the baby out to play only when it feels safe.

gen1.en: If it senses any danger, it will vigorously spray water or a special type of ink from its mouth.

gen2.en: When they're in a safe location, they can be seen playfully tangling their tails together.

gen3.en: By cleverly flicking the fins on its back side to side, it moves in any direction while facing forward. It spits ink to escape if it senses danger.

gen4.en: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

gen1.en: Touching the back fin causes numb­ ness. It hooks its tail to coral to stay in place while sleeping.

gen2.en: The male raises the young. If it is approached, it uses its toxic spikes to fend off the intruder.

gen3.en: The poisonous barbs all over its body are highly valued as ingredients for making traditional herbal medicine. It shows no mercy to anything approaching its nest.

gen4.en: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional medicine ingredients.

gen1.en: When it is time for them to lay eggs, they can be seen swimming up rivers and falls in large groups.

gen2.en: During spawning season, they swim gracefully in the water, searching for their perfect mate.

gen3.en: In the springtime, schools of GOLDEEN can be seen swimming up falls and rivers. It metes out staggering damage with its single horn.

gen4.en: Though it appears very elegant when swimming with fins unfurled, it can jab powerfully with its horn.

gen1.en: It is the male's job to make a nest by carving out boulders in a stream using the horn on its head.

gen2.en: When autumn comes, the males patrol the area around their nests in order to protect their offspring.

gen3.en: It punches holes in boulders on stream­ beds. This is a clever innovation that prevents its eggs from being attacked or washed away by the current.

gen4.en: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

gen1.en: As long as the center section is unharmed, it can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.

gen2.en: When the stars twinkle at night, it floats up from the sea floor, and its body's center core flickers.

gen3.en: It gathers with others in the night and makes its red core glow on and off with the twinkling stars. It can regenerate limbs if they are severed from its body.

gen4.en: If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.

gen1.en: The center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in 7 colors.

gen2.en: It is said that it uses the seven- colored core of its body to send electric waves into outer space.

gen3.en: People in ancient times imagined that STARMIE were transformed from the reflections of stars that twinkled on gentle waves at night.

gen4.en: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

gen1.en: Always practices its pantomime act. It makes enemies believe something exists that really doesn't.

gen2.en: It uses the mysterious power it has in its fingers to solidify air into an invisible wall.

gen3.en: A MR. MIME is a master of pantomime. It can convince others that something unseeable actually exists. Once believed, the imaginary object does become real.

gen4.en: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

gen8.en: The behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.

gen1.en: Leaps out of tall grass and slices prey with its scythes. The move­ ment looks like that of a ninja.

gen2.en: It's very proud of its speed. It moves so fast that its opponent does not even know what knocked it down.

gen3.en: Its blindingly fast speed adds to the sharpness of its twin forearm scythes. The scythes can slice through thick logs in one wicked stroke.

gen4.en: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

gen8.en: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.

gen1.en: Appears to move to a rhythm of its own, as if it were dancing. It wiggles its hips as it walks.

gen2.en: It has several different cry pat­ terns, each of which seems to have its own meaning.

gen3.en: A JYNX sashays rhythmically as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without noticing.

gen4.en: Its cries sound like human speech. However, it is impossible to tell what it is trying to say.

gen1.en: If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this POKéMON has eaten electricity at a power plant.

gen2.en: When two ELECTABUZZ touch, they control the electric currents to communicate their feelings.

gen3.en: When a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.

gen4.en: It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.

gen8.en: Feeds on electrical energy. During sudden showers beneath looming thunderclouds, one can observe Electabuzz scaling tall trees, where the Pokémon will then wait for lightning to strike.

gen1.en: Born in an active volcano. Its body is always cloaked in flames, so it looks like a big ball of fire.

gen2.en: It moves more frequently in hot areas. It can heal itself by dipping its wound into lava.

gen3.en: In battle, it blows out intense flames from all over its body to intimidate its foe. These fiery bursts create heat waves that ignite grass and trees in the area.

gen4.en: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

gen8.en: Legend has it that this Pokémon was born from the crater of a volcano. When wounded, it bathes in lava to heal its body, much as one would soak in a hot spring.

gen1.en: Grips its prey in its pincers and squeezes hard! It can't move if it's cold, so it lives in warm places.

gen2.en: When the tempera­ ture drops at night, it sleeps on treetops or among roots where it is well hidden.

gen3.en: Their pincers are strong enough to shatter thick logs. Because they dislike cold, PINSIR burrow and sleep under the ground on chilly nights.

gen4.en: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can’t tear, it tosses far.

gen1.en: A rowdy POKéMON with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won't stop until it hits something.

gen2.en: These violent POKéMON fight with other mem­ bers of their herd in order to prove their strength.

gen3.en: It is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for TAUROS to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.

gen4.en: Once it takes aim at its foe, it makes a headlong charge. It is famous for its violent nature.

gen1.en: Famous for being very unreliable. It can be found swimming in seas, lakes, rivers and shallow puddles.

gen2.en: This weak and pathetic POKéMON gets easily pushed along rivers when there are strong currents.

gen3.en: Its swimming muscles are weak, so it is easily washed away by currents. In places where water pools, you can see many MAGIKARP deposited there by the flow.

gen4.en: A MAGIKARP living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

gen8.en: A feeble, pitiful imbecile of a Pokémon that is nonetheless very hardy. Unperturbed by turbid water, it can be found living in all sorts of places.

gen4.en: Once it begins to rampage, a GYARADOS will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.

gen1.en: Brutally vicious and enormously destructive. Known for totally destroying cities in ancient times.

gen2.en: It appears when­ ever there is world conflict, burning down any place it travels through.

gen3.en: It is an extremely vicious and violent POKéMON. When humans begin to fight, it will appear and burn everything to the ground with intensely hot flames.

gen8.en: I suspect this Pokémon to be the true identity of a dragon written of in ancient texts, which claimed that it razed an entire village with white-hot beams from its maw.

gen1.en: A gentle soul that can read the minds of people. It can ferry people across the sea on its back.

gen2.en: This gentle POKéMON loves to give people rides and provides a ve­ ry comfortable way to get around.

gen3.en: People have driven LAPRAS almost to the point of extinction. In the evenings, it is said to sing plaintively as it seeks what few others of its kind still remain.

gen4.en: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

gen1.en: When it spots an enemy, its body transfigures into an almost perfect copy of its oppo­ nent.

gen2.en: When it encount­ ers another DITTO, it will move faster than normal to duplicate that opponent exactly.

gen3.en: A DITTO rearranges its cell structure to transform itself. However, if it tries to change based on its memory, it will get details wrong.

gen4.en: It has the ability to reconstitute its entire cellular structure to transform into whatever it sees.

evolutions.condition: with a Fairy-type move and two levels of Affection

gen1.en: Its genetic code is unstable, so it could evolve in a variety of ways. There are only a few alive.

gen2.en: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

gen3.en: An EEVEE has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various STONES causes this POKéMON to evolve.

gen4.en: Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes.

gen8.en: Harbors the potential to evolve into manifold forms. Within Eevee lies the key to the mysteries of Pokémon evolution—I'm certain of it.

gen1.en: Its cell structure is similar to water molecules. It will melt away and become invis­ ible in water.

gen2.en: As it uses the fins on the tip of its tail to swim, it blends with the water perfectly.

gen3.en: VAPOREON underwent a spontaneous mutation and grew fins and gills that allow them to live underwater. They have the ability to freely control water.

gen4.en: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can melt away into water.

gen8.en: Tests show that its cells closely resemble water molecules, which perhaps explains its ability to conceal its form while submerged. I believe the origins of mermaid folklore lie with this Pokémon.

gen1.en: A sensitive POKé­ MON that easily becomes sad or angry. Every time its mood changes, it charges power.

gen2.en: The negatively charged ions generated in its fur create a constant sparking noise.

gen3.en: Its cells generate weak power that is amplified by its fur’s static electricity to drop thunderbolts. The bristling fur is made of electrically charged needles.

gen4.en: If agitated, it uses electricity to straighten out its fur and launch it in small bunches.

gen8.en: Bristles its fur into sharp, needlelike points when enraged. One can hear electricity crackle in its breath when it exhales.

gen1.en: It has a flame chamber inside its body. It inhales, then blows out fire that is over 3,000F degrees.

gen2.en: Once it has stored up enough heat, this POKéMON's body temperature can reach up to 1700 degrees.

gen3.en: FLAREON’s fluffy fur releases heat into the air so that its body does not get excessively hot. Its body temperature can rise to a maximum of 1,650 degrees F.

gen4.en: Inhaled air is heated in the flame sac in its body to an intense fire over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

gen8.en: Flames burn within a saclike organ inside this Pokémon. When Flareon inhales, these flames grow in intensity, reaching a mighty 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

gen1.en: The only POKéMON people anticipate can fly into space. None has managed the feat yet, however.

gen2.en: An artificial POKéMON created due to extensive research, it can perform only what is in its program.

gen3.en: It is capable of reverting itself entirely back to program data in order to enter cyberspace. A PORYGON is copy- protected so it cannot be duplicated.

gen4.en: A man-made Pokémon created using advanced scientific means. It can move freely in cyberspace.

gen8.en: It has no discernible heartbeat and does not seem to draw breath, and yet it appears to function without issue. I cannot even begin to explain this utterly bizarre anomaly.

gen1.en: An ancient POKéMON that was recovered from a fossil. It swims by cleverly twisting its 10 tentacles about.

gen2.en: In prehistoric times, it swam on the sea floor, eating plankton. Its fossils are sometimes found.

gen3.en: One of the ancient and long-since-extinct POKéMON that have been regenerated from fossils by humans. If attacked, it withdraws into its hard shell.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.

gen1.en: Sharp beaks ring its mouth. Its shell was too big for it to move freely, so it became extinct.

gen2.en: Its heavy shell allowed it to reach only nearby food. This could be the reason it is extinct.

gen3.en: An OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large, making its movements slow and ponderous.

gen4.en: It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.

gen1.en: A POKéMON that was recovered from a fossil. It uses the eyes on its back while hiding on the sea floor.

gen2.en: Three hundred million years ago, it hid on the sea floor. It also has eyes on its back that glow.

gen3.en: It is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in rare cases, living examples have been discovered. KABUTO have not changed for 300 million years.

gen4.en: It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.

gen1.en: A slim and fast swimmer. It slices its prey with its sharp sickles and drinks the body fluids.

gen2.en: It was able to swim quickly thro­ ugh the water by compactly folding up its razor-sharp sickles.

gen3.en: KABUTOPS once swam underwater to hunt for prey. It was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from changes in its gills and legs.

gen4.en: It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.

gen1.en: A savage POKéMON that died out in ancient times. It was resurrected using DNA taken from amber.

gen2.en: In prehistoric times, this POKéMON flew freely and fearlessly through the skies.

gen3.en: AERODACTYL is a POKéMON from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from DNA extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

gen1.en: Will eat anything, even if the food happens to be a little moldy. It never gets an upset stomach.

gen2.en: This POKéMON's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

gen3.en: SNORLAX’s typical day consists of nothing more than eating and sleeping. It is such a docile POKéMON that there are children who use its big belly as a place to play.

gen4.en: When its belly is full, it becomes too lethargic to even lift a finger, so it is safe to bounce on its belly.

gen8.en: This glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.

gen1.en: A legendary bird POKéMON. It freezes water that is contained in winter air and makes it snow.

gen2.en: Legendary bird POKéMON. As it flies through the sky, it cools the air, causing snow to fall.

gen3.en: ARTICUNO is a legendary bird POKéMON that can control ice. The flapping of its wings chills the air. As a result, it is said that when this POKéMON flies, snow will fall.

gen4.en: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

gen1.en: This legendary bird POKéMON is said to appear when the sky turns dark and lightning showers down.

gen2.en: Legendary bird POKéMON. They say lightning caused by the flapping of its wings causes summer storms.

gen3.en: ZAPDOS is a legendary bird POKéMON that has the ability to control electricity. It usually lives in thunderclouds. It gains power if it is stricken by lightning bolts.

gen4.en: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

gen1.en: A legendary bird POKéMON. As it flaps its flaming wings, even the night sky will turn red.

gen2.en: Legendary bird POKéMON. It is said to migrate from the south along with the spring.

gen3.en: MOLTRES is a legendary bird POKéMON that can control fire. If injured, it is said to dip its body in the molten magma of a volcano to burn and heal itself.

gen4.en: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

gen1.en: The existence of this mythical POKéMON was only recently confirmed by a fisherman who caught one.

gen2.en: It sheds many lay­ ers of skin as it grows larger. Dur­ ing this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

gen3.en: A DRATINI continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.

gen4.en: It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.

gen1.en: According to a witness, its body was surrounded by a strange aura that gave it a mystical look.

gen2.en: It is called the divine POKéMON. When its entire body brightens slightly, the weather changes.
