gen3.en: A DRAGONAIR stores an enormous amount of energy inside its body. It is said to alter the weather around it by loosing energy from the crystals on its neck and tail.

gen4.en: If its body takes on an aura, the weather changes instantly. It is said to live in seas and lakes.

gen1.en: It is said that this POKéMON lives somewhere in the sea and that it flies. However, it is only a rumor.

gen2.en: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

gen3.en: It can circle the globe in just 16 hours. It is a kindhearted POKéMON that leads lost and foundering ships in a storm to the safety of land.

gen4.en: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

gen1.en: Its DNA is almost the same as MEW's. However, its size and disposition are vastly dif­ ferent.

gen2.en: Said to rest qui­ etly in an undiscovered cave, this POKéMON was created solely for battling.

gen3.en: A POKéMON that was created by genetic manipulation. However, even though the scientific power of humans made its body, they failed to give it a warm heart.

gen4.en: A Pokémon created by recombining MEW’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

gen1.en: When viewed through a micro­ scope, this POKéMON's short, fine, delicate hair can be seen.

gen2.en: Because it can learn any move, some people began research to see if it is the ancestor of all POKéMON.

gen3.en: A MEW is said to possess the genes of all POKéMON. It is capable of making itself invisible at will, so it entirely avoids notice even if it approaches people.

gen4.en: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe MEW to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

gen2.en: It loves to bask in the sunlight. It uses the leaf on its head to seek out warm places.

gen3.en: It waves its leaf around to keep foes at bay. However, a sweet fragrance also wafts from the leaf, creating a friendly atmosphere that becalms the battlers.

gen4.en: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

gen2.en: The scent that wafts from the leaves on its neck causes anyone who smells it to become energetic.

gen3.en: A BAYLEEF’s neck is ringed by curled-up leaves. Inside each leaf is a small tree shoot. The fragrance of this shoot makes people peppy.

gen4.en: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

gen2.en: Anyone who stands beside it becomes refreshed, just as if they were relaxing in a sunny forest.

gen3.en: The fragrance of a MEGANIUM’s flower soothes and calms emotions. In battle, it gives off more of its becalming scent to blunt the foe’s fighting spirit.

gen4.en: Its breath has the fantastic ability to revive dead plants and flowers.

gen2.en: The fire that spouts from its back burns hottest when it is angry. The flaring flames intimidate foes.

gen3.en: It flares flames from its back to protect itself. The fire burns vigorously if the POKéMON is angry. When it is tired, it sputters with incomplete combustion.

gen4.en: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

gen8.en: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.

gen2.en: Before battle, it turns its back on its opponent to demonstrate how ferociously its fire blazes.

gen3.en: It intimidates foes with intense gusts of flames and superheated air. Its quick nimbleness lets it dodge attacks even while scorching an enemy.

gen4.en: It intimidates foes with the heat of its flames. The fire burns more strongly when it readies to fight.

gen8.en: This creature's fur is most mysterious—it is wholly impervious to the burning touch of flame. Should Quilava turn its back to you, take heed! Such a posture indicates a forthcoming attack.

gen2.en: When heat from its body causes the air around it to shimmer, this is a sign that it is ready to battle.

gen3.en: It can hide behind a shimmering heat haze that it creates using its intense flames. TYPHLOSION create blazing explosive blasts that burn everything to cinders.

gen4.en: It attacks using blasts of fire. It creates heat shimmers with intense fire to hide itself.

gen8.en: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui's center.

gen2.en: This rough critter chomps at any moving object it sees. Turning your back on it is not recommended.

gen3.en: Despite its small body, TOTODILE’s jaws are very powerful. While it may think it is just playfully nipping, its bite has enough strength to cause serious injury.

gen4.en: It has the habit of biting anything with its developed jaws. Even its Trainer needs to be careful.

gen2.en: The tips of its fangs are slanted backward. Once those fangs clamp down, the prey has no hope of escape.

gen3.en: Once its jaws clamp down on its foe, it will absolutely not let go. Because the tips of its fangs are forked back like fishhooks, they become irremovably embedded.

gen4.en: Once it bites down, it won’t let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

gen2.en: Although it has a massive body, its powerful hind legs enable it to move quickly, even on the ground.

gen3.en: It opens its huge mouth to intimidate enemies. In battle, it runs using its thick and powerful hind legs to charge the foe with incredible speed.

gen4.en: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

gen2.en: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

gen3.en: They take turns standing guard when it is time to sleep. The sentry awakens the others if it senses danger. If one becomes separated, it turns sleepless with fear.

gen4.en: It has a very nervous nature. It stands up high on its tail so it can scan wide areas.

gen2.en: It lives in narrow burrows that fit its slim body. The deeper the nests go, the more maze­ like they become.

gen3.en: A FURRET has a very slim build. When under attack, it can squirm through narrow spaces and get away. In spite of its short limbs, it is very nimble and fleet.

gen4.en: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

gen2.en: It begins to hoot at the same time every day. Some trainers use them in place of clocks.

gen3.en: It has an internal organ that senses the earth’s rotation. Using this special organ, a HOOTHOOT begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.

gen4.en: It marks time precisely. Some countries consider it to be a wise friend, versed in the world’s ways.

gen2.en: Its extremely soft feathers make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.

gen3.en: It unfailingly catches prey in darkness. NOCTOWL owe their success to superior vision that allows them to see in minimal light, and to their supple and silent wings.

gen4.en: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

gen2.en: It is timid and clusters together with others. The fluid secreted by its feet indicates its location.

gen3.en: LEDYBA communicate using a fluid that they secrete from where the legs join the body. They are said to convey feelings to others by altering the fluid’s scent.

gen4.en: It is so timid, it can’t move if it isn’t with a swarm of others. It conveys its feelings with scent.

gen2.en: In the daytime when it gets warm, it curls up inside a big leaf and drifts off into a deep slumber.

gen3.en: It is said that in lands with clean air, where the stars fill the sky, there live many LEDIAN. For good reason, they use the light of the stars as energy.

gen4.en: It uses starlight as energy. When more stars appear at night, the patterns on its back grow larger.

gen2.en: If prey becomes ensnared in its nest of spun string, it waits motionlessly until it becomes dark.

gen3.en: The web it spins can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that a SPINARAK determines its prey by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web.

gen4.en: It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.

gen2.en: Rather than mak­ ing a nest in one specific spot, it wanders in search of food after darkness falls.

gen3.en: Its feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. It constricts its foe with thin and strong silk webbing.

gen4.en: It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.

gen2.en: As a result of its pursuit of faster, yet more silent flight, a new set of wings grew on its hind legs.

gen3.en: Over the course of evolution, its hind legs turned into wings. By alternately resting its front and rear wings, it can fly all day without having to stop.

gen4.en: The transformation of its legs into wings made it better at flying, but more clumsy at walking.

gen8.en: Its hind limbs have become another set of wings. Crobat expertly maneuvers its four wings to dart in exquisite fashion through even the most confined caves without losing any speed.

gen2.en: Its antennae, whi­ ch evolved from a fin, have both po­ sitive and neg­ ative charges flo­ wing through them.

gen3.en: When it senses danger, it discharges positive and negative electricity from its two antennae. It lives in depths beyond sunlight’s reach.

gen4.en: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

gen2.en: This POKéMON uses the bright part of its body, which changed from a dorsal fin, to lure prey.

gen3.en: The light-emitting orbs on its back are very bright. They are formed from a part of its dorsal fin. This POKéMON illuminates the inky darkness of deep seas.

gen4.en: LANTURN’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”

gen2.en: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

gen3.en: It is still inept at retaining electricity. When it is startled, it discharges power accidentally. It gets better at holding power as it grows older.

gen4.en: The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.

gen8.en: Pichu stores electricity in the sacs on its cheeks but discharges it inadvertently when agitated or excited. Being yet immature, the Pokémon's handling of electricity is rather inept.

gen2.en: If the impact site of a meteorite is found, this POKéMON is certain to be within the immediate area.

gen3.en: On nights with many shooting stars, CLEFFA can be seen dancing in a ring. They dance until daybreak, when they quench their thirst with the morning dew.

gen4.en: It is often seen when shooting stars fill the night skies. It’s said to arrive riding on a shooting star.

gen4.en: It is said that kids who drink MILTANK’s milk grow up to become hearty, healthy adults.

gen2.en: Biting into one of the delicious eggs that BLISSEY provides will make everyone around smile with joy.

gen4.en: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

gen8.en: A kindhearted Pokémon that will care for any sick person or Pokémon until their health improves. The eggs it lays are delicious and bring good fortune to those who eat them.

gen2.en: This rough POKéMON stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

gen3.en: RAIKOU embodies the speed of lightning. Its roars send shock waves shuddering through the air and ground as if lightning bolts were crashing down.

gen4.en: It is said to have fallen with lightning. It can fire thunderbolts from the rain clouds on its back.

gen2.en: This brawny POKéMON courses around the earth, spouting flames hotter than a volcano's magma.

gen4.en: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

gen2.en: This divine POKéMON blows around the world, always in search of a pure reservoir.

gen3.en: SUICUNE embodies the compassion of a pure spring of water. It runs across the land with gliding elegance. It has the power to purify dirty water.

gen4.en: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

gen2.en: Born deep under­ ground, this POKéMON becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

gen3.en: A LARVITAR is born deep under the ground. It must eat its way through the soil above and reach the surface for it to see its parents’ faces.

gen4.en: A Pokémon that eats soil. Once it has eaten a large mountain, it goes to sleep so it can grow.

gen2.en: It will not stay still, even while it's a pupa. It already has arms and legs under its solid shell.

gen3.en: A PUPITAR creates a gas inside its body that it ejects under compression to propel itself like a jet. Its body can withstand a collision with solid steel.

gen2.en: In just one of its mighty hands, it has the power to make the ground shake and moun­ tains crumble.

gen2.en: It has an incred­ ible ability to calm raging sto­ rms. It is said that LUGIA appears when storms start.

gen4.en: It sleeps in a deep-sea trench. If it flaps its wings, it is said to cause a 40-day storm.

gen2.en: It will reveal itself before a pure-hearted trainer by shining its bright rain­ bow-colored wings.

gen4.en: It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

gen4.en: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

gen4.en: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

gen8.en: A poison wielder with a dastardly personality. Despite such qualities, this species is afforded a measure of popularity due to its peculiar cry and comical features.

gen4.en: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.

gen8.en: Its crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.

gen4.en: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet- smelling drool and gulps them down.

gen8.en: Though this is a plant Pokémon, it has a gluttonous and unruly temperament. Carnivine attacks its prey with its cavernous maw wide open.

gen4.en: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

gen8.en: What a gorgeous sight this Pokémon is as it swims with its long, pink-painted caudal fins fluttering behind it. Finneon's beautiful appearance has led to its nickname: ”finery fish.”

gen4.en: It crawls along the seafloor using its long front fins like legs. It competes for food with LANTURN.

gen8.en: Uses its gleaming fins to hunt its prey. The view of Lumineon schooling near the surface of the sea at night is breathtaking— it's as though there were shining stars right there.

gen4.en: People organize tours to see this Pokémon frolic and skim the tops of waves with REMORAID.

gen8.en: Though ball-like in shape, this Pokémon is a proficient swimmer. I have discovered that if a Mantyke spends much time with schools of Remoraid, it will eventually achieve evolution.

gen4.en: Seemingly curious about people, they gather around footsteps they find on snowy mountains.

gen8.en: One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.

gen4.en: They appear when the snow flowers bloom. When the petals fall, they retreat to places unknown again.

gen8.en: A powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.

gen4.en: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

gen8.en: This species corners prey as a pack, under the guidance of a leader. Weavile displays increased cunning, leading me to speculate that its evolution caused further brain development.

gen4.en: A group tried to use scientific means to make MAGNEZONE evolve, but their efforts ended in failure.

gen8.en: I theorize that a special magnetic field influenced this Pokémon, changing its molecular structure and causing it to evolve. It emits strange radio waves toward space from its antenna.

gen4.en: The long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness.

gen8.en: Its tongue can extend and contract freely, and it is capable of reaching lengths over 10 times Lickilicky's height. Beware of the saliva, as it contains corrosive elements.

gen4.en: It can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening then expanding muscles instantly.

gen8.en: This Pokémon evolved through use of a curious item. Its rocklike hide is composed of a mysterious substance and can withstand a blow from a masterwork sword with nary a scratch.

gen4.en: Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.

gen8.en: Draped with long vines, it resembles a shrub in appearance. It swings bundles of vines as though they were arms, wrapping them around prey to ensnare them.

gen4.en: Heedless of enemy attacks, it closes in, shoves its tails onto the foe, then looses high voltage.

gen8.en: Its evolution was induced by an unusual item, and its electrical output rises along with its heart rate. From its tails, it can unleash an electric current measuring 20,000 volts.

gen4.en: When launching 3,600 degrees F fireballs, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.

gen8.en: Use of a strange item caused this Pokémon to evolve. Fireballs launched from the ends of its tubelike arms are hot enough to melt an iron pot in an instant.

gen4.en: It shares many blessings with people who respect one another’s rights and avoid needless strife.

gen8.en: Scant few have ever sighted this Pokémon. After studying what literature remains, I am certain Togekiss will reveal itself when peace reigns in the land.

gen4.en: Its jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

gen8.en: Extremely violent. When hunting, it wastes none of its energy, aiming only for prey's most vulnerable spots. Any who manage to tame this Pokémon must be of incredible bravery.

gen4.en: It basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.

gen8.en: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.

gen4.en: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

gen8.en: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.

gen4.en: If it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.

gen8.en: It glides soundlessly on pitch-black wings and sinks sharp fangs into the throat of its prey. It takes on a look of satisfaction once it has entirely drained its prey of blood.

gen4.en: A frozen one was dug up from soil dating back 10,000 years. It woke up to much amazement.

gen8.en: This species reached its zenith during the period known as the ice age. I suspect that Hisui's frigid climate is in harmony with Mamoswine's constitution, thus awakening hidden potential.

gen4.en: Its programming was modified to enable work in alien dimensions. It did not work as planned.

gen8.en: A curious item induced this evolution. The Pokémon's offensive capabilities have greatly increased, but the strangeness of its behavior has magnified in equal measure. This worries me.

gen4.en: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

gen8.en: The blades extending from its elbows are sharper than the finest swords. Its swordsmanship, albeit self-taught, is astonishingly impressive.

gen4.en: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

gen8.en: It is able to emit powerful magnetism, allowing it control over the iron sand that forms its luscious mustache. Using this iron sand, Probopass forms hard stones with which it smites its prey.

gen4.en: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

gen8.en: Comes to those whose lives have come to an end and escorts their souls to the afterlife. Known to mistakenly take the souls of those who yet have life left in them, albeit rarely.

gen4.en: It freezes prey by blowing its -58 degrees F breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

gen8.en: A Pokémon inhabited by the soul of a woman who died bearing a grudge in the snowy mountains. Legends of Froslass placing deathly curses on misbehaving men send shivers down my spine.

gen4.en: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

gen8.en: This bizarre Pokémon appears to be a will-o'-the-wisp powered by electricity. Be wary, as Rotom is both smart and mischievous.

gen4.en: When UXIE flew, people gained the ability to solve problems. It was the birth of knowledge.

gen8.en: A Pokémon feared but also respected for stealing away the memories of evildoers. I have found records that suggest Uxie holds dominion over knowledge.

gen4.en: When MESPRIT flew, people learned the joy and sadness of living. It was the birth of emotions.

gen8.en: Known as the Being of Emotion. In legend, this Pokémon was feared, as any who showed disrespect would have their emotions thrown into disarray.

gen4.en: When AZELF flew, people gained the determination to do things. It was the birth of willpower.

gen8.en: The dreaded Being of Willpower. Legends tell of this Pokémon manipulating the will of its adversaries and turning them into puppets of its own.

gen4.en: A legendary Pokémon of Sinnoh. It is said that time flows when DIALGA’s heart beats.

gen8.en: This Pokémon is revered as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Dialga was what caused the vast river of time to begin flowing in our world.

gen4.en: A legendary Pokémon of Sinnoh. It is said that space becomes more stable with PALKIA’s every breath.

gen8.en: This Pokémon is feared as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Palkia was what caused the walls of our world to disappear, creating a sky that spans for infinity.

gen4.en: Its body is made of rugged steel. However, it is partially melted in spots because of its own heat.

gen8.en: Stories tell of this Pokémon being birthed from the boiling magma within Mount Coronet. Its molten-steel body holds many mysteries.

gen4.en: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

gen8.en: According to legend, Regigigas pulled landmasses together and bound them with rope to create the continent of Hisui. Though I have my doubts, the story could well contain a shred of truth.

gen4.en: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

gen8.en: There is one Hisuian verse that tells of a powerful light creating a deep shadow. I imagine that this deep shadow is Giratina.

gen4.en: On nights around the quarter moon, the aurora from its tail extends and undulates beautifully.

gen8.en: Cresselia is reminiscent of the crescent moon. It leaves a brilliant line of light in its wake as it flies across the night sky. I daresay it resembles the heavenly maiden who created the Milky Way.

gen4.en: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

gen8.en: Can be seen floating offshore during seasons when the seas are warm. Its azure body blends in with the ocean waters—logic suggests this is a defense mechanism against natural predators.

gen4.en: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

gen8.en: Rumored to migrate across the oceans and visit Hisui's coastal waters only rarely. Although Manaphy resembles Phione, it is also quite different. The relation between the two is unclear.

gen4.en: To protect itself, it afflicts those around it with nightmares. However, it means no harm.

gen8.en: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.

gen4.en: The flowers all over its body burst into bloom if it is lovingly hugged and senses gratitude.

gen8.en: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.

gen4.en: It is said to have emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing, then shaped the world.

gen8.en: It is the heavenly fount from which pours the light that shines across Hisui. Its luminance guides and protects all Pokémon. Hisuian mythology states that Arceus is the creator of all things.
This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name ”scalchop”.
Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.
Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.
The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.
I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.
Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.
A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.
With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.

evolutions.condition: Defeat 3 Bisharp leading Pawniard and level-up

gen8.en: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.
Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.
This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one.
They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil.
When the incarnations of wind and of lightning clash, Landorus arrives to quell the conflict. After the tempests and thunderbolts abate, the land is sure to be blessed with bountiful harvests.

evolutions.condition: level 32, with a Dark-type in the party
level 30, with the console turned upside-down

gen8.en: It emits a soothing aura from its ribbon-shaped organs. It wraps these appendages around quarrelers to instantly restore calm to the situation.
Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.
A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.
The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.
Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.
The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.
The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.
A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.
I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.
Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.
Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.
When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

evolutions.condition: Level up with 999 Coins in the bag
